The film purports to be part of a \"mosa.theme: the dangers of unchecked power; the blurring of boundaries between self and others; the instability of non-traditional relationships

While J. D. Cahill (John Wayne), a widow.coa: A U.S. Marshal is away from home, leaving two sons who aid a group of prisoners escape from jail and rob a bank, killing the town's sheriff during the robbery. The younger son hides the stolen money while the rest of the gang returns to their cells as an alibi. The Marshal returns home and begins investigating the crime with the help of a tracker, leading them to arrest four innocent suspects who are later found guilty and scheduled for hanging.
While J. D. Cahill (John Wayne), a widow.outcomes: Protagonist resolves conflict through justice and protects his sons from harm. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as Cahill learns to trust his sons and they learn responsibility.
While J. D. Cahill (John Wayne), a widow.theme: justice vs vigilantism; loyalty and redemption; father-son relationships

When Beth (Mitchell) opens her remote ro.coa:
A remote roadside diner opens for business.
Almost immediately, a handsome drifter bursts in, bleeding and on the run.
The drifter claims three surfers are chasing him.
The surfers appear at the diner.
When Beth (Mitchell) opens her remote ro.outcomes: Protagonist survives and maintains diner's stability despite chaos. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing danger from unknown pursuers.
When Beth (Mitchell) opens her remote ro.theme: trust vs instinct; loyalty under pressure; survival in uncertainty

The film tells of Juan Desouza, a lawyer.coa:
A lawyer embarks on a one-day business trip to the countryside.
He discovers that the man traveling next to him is dead, rather than sleeping.
Secretly, he assumes the dead man's identity and invents a profession for himself.
He finds a place to stay in the village where the dead man used to live.
The film tells of Juan Desouza, a lawyer.outcomes: Protagonist returns home with a newfound sense of self-discovery. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and a shift in perspective.

Three young men (Jacques, Pierre and Mic.coa: A friend of one of them tells Jacques he has a compromising package to deliver and asks him to leave it at their place discreetly. Jacques agrees and leaves for Japan on a one-month trip, telling Pierre and Michel about the package. One of Jacques' former girlfriends drops off a baby before their door, leading Pierre and Michel to believe it is the package they are waiting for. They soon realize that the baby is not the package, but rather a child left at their doorstep.
Three young men (Jacques, Pierre and Mic.outcomes: Protagonists face failure in their carefree lives due to the arrival of the baby and the consequences of Jacques' actions. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threats from gangsters and the need for personal transformation.
Three young men (Jacques, Pierre and Mic.theme: responsibility; chaos vs order; moral accountability

Daniel is an odd guy who lives with his.coa: Daniel lives with his quarrelling parents and keeps a distance from others due to feeling different. He has no friends and is approaching his 19th birthday, which coincides with graduation preparations. Adam, Daniel's classteacher, lives in a relationship with David, keeping a secret behind a locked door. A life-threatening situation occurs when Daniel and Adam find themselves together in the dark, cut off from the outside world.
Daniel is an odd guy who lives with his.outcomes: Protagonist Daniel survives the ordeal with newfound connection to others. The conflict is unresolved with deeper issues remaining.
Daniel is an odd guy who lives with his.theme: isolation vs connection; hidden truths; survival under adversity

Ringo, Frank and their sidekick Tom are.coa: Ringo, Frank, and Tom are hired by Walton to rescue his daughter. They successfully shoot their way through a band of Mexicans and complete the rescue. After the rescue, Jane marries Ringo, causing tension in the friendship between Ringo and Frank. The two do not meet again until after the American Civil War ends.
Ringo, Frank and their sidekick Tom are.outcomes: Protagonist achieves personal success and stability as a town sheriff. The conflict is unresolved due to the antagonistic actions of Frank, who obtains wealth but also perpetuates violence and harm.
Ringo, Frank and their sidekick Tom are.theme: friendship vs loyalty; redemption through service; consequences of past actions

Wilhelm Lehmann is informed that he will.coa: Wilhelm Lehmann receives news that he will receive an award on his sixty-fifth birthday. His children are informed of this, but none of them can attend to honor him and their mother Auguste. A series of comical mistakes ensue, causing chaos and disrupting the planned celebration. The family eventually arrives at the ceremony, where Wilhelm is awarded the Order.
Wilhelm Lehmann is informed that he will.outcomes: Protagonist feels honored and celebrated by family on his special day. The conflict is resolved with a joyful family reunion and traditional celebration.
Wilhelm Lehmann is informed that he will.theme: family bonding; generational values; recognition of hard work

Moderately successful criminal defense a.coa: A moderately successful criminal defense attorney operates around Los Angeles County out of a Lincoln Town Car driven by a former client working off his legal fees. The attorney takes on a new case involving a wealthy realtor accused of assault and attempted murder, who initially appears to be innocent and set up by the female "victim". As the case unfolds, the realtor's lies and surprising revelations change the attorney's original case theory. This leads him to reconsider the situation of Jesus Menendez, a former client serving time in prison after pleading guilty to a similar crime.
Moderately successful criminal defense a.outcomes: Protagonist successfully defends client and resolves conflict, but also acquires emotional baggage. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and professional growth.
Moderately successful criminal defense a.theme: justice vs corruption; redemption through truth; the blurred lines between right and wrong

The book begins with a fictional corresp.coa:
The author receives a letter from his publisher encouraging him to write another light and cheerful love story.
The author replies that he can offer a summer story instead.
The author, now referred to as Peter, begins narrating his own story in first person about a summer vacation with his friend Lydia, nicknamed "die Prinzessin".
They travel by train and ferry to Sweden.
The book begins with a fictional corresp.outcomes: Protagonist Kurt's story is told in a lighthearted tone, with no major conflicts resolved. The conflict of the suffering child is partially resolved with personal transformation through intervention.
The book begins with a fictional corresp.theme: summer romance; social commentary; human connection

Bassi and Edwin are the two laziest, mos.coa:
Bassi and Edwin fall in love with their neighbors Thea and Susi.
They pretend to be reliable young men to impress them.
To keep up appearances, they accompany their friends to a meeting of the local chapter house.
At the meeting, they are forced to undertake several duties that they would not have done otherwise.
Bassi and Edwin are the two laziest, mos.outcomes: Protagonists achieve a successful relationship with their girlfriends despite initial pretenses. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation and social acceptance.
Bassi and Edwin are the two laziest, mos.theme: social facade; love vs appearance; personal growth through relationships

Sezim (Alina Turdumamatova) wants to ful.coa:
A young woman gets kidnapped by a group of men and taken to the hinterland.
The kidnappers force her to marry one of them against her will.
She is threatened with social stigmatization if she refuses the marriage.
She becomes desperate to find a way out.
Sezim (Alina Turdumamatova) wants to ful.outcomes: Protagonist manages to escape her forced marriage and returns to city. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader cultural constraints remain.
Sezim (Alina Turdumamatova) wants to ful.theme: freedom vs tradition; cultural oppression; female agency

Gold-mine operator \"Boss\" Kruger (Raym.coa: A group of fugitives, including two individuals, are forced to work in a gold mine under harsh conditions. The mine's operator exercises total control over the workers, treating them like prisoners. One of the workers befriends an outsider, a lawyer who has arrived at the mine with a hidden agenda. The lawyer begins to investigate the mine and its operator.
Gold-mine operator \"Boss\" Kruger (Raym.outcomes: Protagonist succeeds in exposing antagonist's crimes, leading to his downfall. The conflict is resolved with justice served and the rule of law upheld.
Gold-mine operator \"Boss\" Kruger (Raym.theme: justice vs oppression; corruption exposed; power dynamics

Jo Jones works in an airplane factory an.coa: Jo Jones works in an airplane factory and longs for the day when she will see her husband again. Her husband leaves for overseas duty, and they have a heart-wrenching farewell at the train station. With their husbands off fighting in World War II, Jo and her co-workers struggle to pay living expenses. They decide to pool their money and rent a house together.
Jo Jones works in an airplane factory an.outcomes: Protagonist is left widowed but finds solace in her new family. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation, but broader emotional pain remains.
Jo Jones works in an airplane factory an.theme: resilience; community support; grief and loss

A young woman, Joana Prats, suffers from.coa: A young woman suffers from agnosia, a primary visual disease that affects her brain's ability to interpret stimuli. She possesses knowledge of an industrial secret left behind by her late father. Her captors discover that she has this knowledge and plan to use her sensory condition to extract the information they want. They kidnap her in order to carry out their sinister plan.
A young woman, Joana Prats, suffers from.outcomes: Protagonist resists exploitation and ultimately thwarts the antagonists' plan. The conflict is resolved with personal resilience but broader threats remain.
A young woman, Joana Prats, suffers from.theme: exploitation of vulnerability; perception vs reality; human resistance

Upper-middle class Mathieu, is spending.coa: Mathieu spends his summer vacation on the French coast before beginning studies as an architect. His mother is deeply depressed due to a family tragedy, and her sister cares for her while Mathieu tries to navigate his own life with his moody younger sister. He meets Cédric at the beach, who is looking for a romantic partner, and they start a romance. This new relationship causes Mathieu to become secretive and spend long hours away from home, sparking curiosity in both his sister and aunt.
Upper-middle class Mathieu, is spending.outcomes: Protagonist survives emotional turmoil and begins healing process. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relational improvement.
Upper-middle class Mathieu, is spending.theme: healing; identity formation; love vs societal expectations

A young Tunisian woman is raped by polic.coa:
A young Tunisian woman is raped by police officers.
She seeks assistance in hospitals to register the crime while still under shock from the attack.
The hospital staff shows indifference towards her situation, leading her to seek help at a police station instead.
At the police station, she encounters hostility and bureaucratic impediments that hinder her ability to file a complaint.
A young Tunisian woman is raped by polic.outcomes: Protagonist is able to register the crime and potentially seek justice. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation, but broader systemic issues remain.
A young Tunisian woman is raped by polic.theme: survival under adversity; justice vs oppression; human resilience

In ancient Korea, Yi Gwak is a former ch.coa: Yi Gwak flees the town after being betrayed and poisoned by the villagers he saved from demons. He passes out in an abandoned shrine and awakens in Midheaven, where he encounters the spirit of Yon-hwa, now going by So-hwa. So-hwa has voluntarily discarded her memories and suffering to assume a new name and title. Yi Gwak also meets Ban-chu, who reveals his plan for a demonic rebellion to invade the living world.
In ancient Korea, Yi Gwak is a former ch.outcomes: Protagonist survives and protects So-hwa from harm. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threats from Ban-chu's forces.
In ancient Korea, Yi Gwak is a former ch.theme: loyalty vs duty; redemption; human resilience

Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson come out.coa: A mysterious murder occurs. The Scowrers, an American criminal organisation, asks Professor Moriarty to exact revenge on John Douglas, who had sent them to prison by informing on them. Holmes and Watson come out of retirement to investigate the murder. They discover that Moriarty is involved in the case.
Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson come out.outcomes: Protagonist (Holmes) successfully resolves the case and defeats the antagonist (Moriarty). The conflict is resolved with a sense of justice restored, but broader threats from The Scowrers remain.
Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson come out.theme: justice vs evil; loyalty and partnership; intellectual superiority

Ivan Warding is a thief who specializes.coa: A wealthy socialite commissions a high-end art piece, which becomes the target of a planned heist. The thief and the con-woman form an alliance to steal the art piece in exchange for freedom from their respective obligations. They devise a plan to infiltrate the socialite's mansion during a charity gala. The thief gains access to the mansion by posing as a caterer, while the con-woman poses as a guest.
Ivan Warding is a thief who specializes.outcomes: Protagonist achieves freedom from his obligation and starts anew. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as they leave their old life behind.

Fish are dying in Chile's rivers due to.coa:
Fish are dying in rivers due to water pollution caused by human activity.
Their lifeless bodies are being dragged ashore along the banks of a river.
A woman emerges from the depths of the river, gasping for air.
She had been left for dead many years ago and returns to her family's dairy farm.
Her husband suffers a heart attack upon seeing her.
His daughter returns home with her two children to take care of him.
The people she thought were dead behave very strangely in her presence.
Electrical appliances also appear to be affected by her return.
Fish are dying in Chile's rivers due to.outcomes: Protagonist (Magdalena) reunites with her family, but they are affected by her presence in strange ways. The conflict is unresolved as the underlying issue of water pollution remains.

For six years Jean has been having an af.coa: Jean and Catherine have an affair for six years. Jean initially hopes that their relationship will lead to a divorce and marriage, but he becomes disillusioned with the lack of progress in his life and their relationship. As a result, Jean's partner meets a divorced man who has a good job and enjoys foreign travel. She agrees to marry him.
For six years Jean has been having an af.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a new relationship and escapes the failed affair. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation through ending an unhappy relationship.
For six years Jean has been having an af.theme: unfulfilled desire; deception vs honesty; moving on

Starting with the birth of the Jesus chi.coa:
The Virgin Mary gives birth to twins.
Only one of the twins is portrayed in the story.
The dark agents (the Four Evangelists) are introduced and begin to shadow the life of the One twin.
The One twin develops a fondness for drinking blood at the Last Supper.
Starting with the birth of the Jesus chi.outcomes: Jesus survives and becomes immortal as a vampire. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation but broader implications of his new nature remain.
Starting with the birth of the Jesus chi.theme: divine nature; free will vs destiny; the struggle for power

The protagonist takes a trip across the.coa: The protagonist takes a trip across the Goltva river on Easter Eve. He engages in conversation with a monk ferryman who is deeply shaken by the recent death of his best friend and mentor. The ferryman reveals that his late friend was a genius master of Akathist, but had no audience or listeners during his lifetime, except for the ferryman himself. The protagonist returns across the river later, disturbed by the contrast between the joyful church service and the ferryman's grief.
The protagonist takes a trip across the.outcomes: Protagonist is unchanged but gains insight into human suffering. The conflict is unresolved with a focus on emotional resonance.
The protagonist takes a trip across the.theme: loneliness; grief; contrast between joy and sorrow

Forced to leave France, Dédée and her bu.coa:
Dédée and her pimp are forced to leave France and arrive in Antwerp.
They settle into their new life, with Dédée working at René's bar and Marco doing drug deals as the doorman.
While on a stroll by the docks, Dédée meets Francesco, an Italian captain who knows René.
Francesco visits the bar later that night to discuss a secret deal with René.
Forced to leave France, Dédée and her bu.outcomes: Protagonist Dédée achieves escape from her abusive situation with the help of ally René. The conflict is resolved with Marco's death, but broader threats remain as Dédée's past continues to haunt her.
Forced to leave France, Dédée and her bu.theme: escape from oppression; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Terra Nostra is divided into three parts.coa: The novel begins with an event in Paris on July 14, 1999. This event sets off a chain reaction that leads to the rebirth of main characters in different ages. The characters are reborn in the 16th century, specifically around the unfinished El Escorial, where they must navigate their new surroundings and interact with one another.
Terra Nostra is divided into three parts.outcomes: Protagonist survives multiple rebirths and transformations. The conflict is unresolved with broader threats remaining.

In 1953, Joe Morelli is traveling rural.coa: Joe Morelli travels rural Sicily offering screen tests for a fee, claiming to work for big Roman film studios. He meets several people who express their deepest feelings in front of the camera. Joe meets Beata, a convent girl, at one of his stops and they form a bond despite his initial protestations. As their relationship deepens, Joe's true intentions are exposed, leading to him being beaten and arrested.
In 1953, Joe Morelli is traveling rural.outcomes: Protagonist survives imprisonment and maintains a connection with his former love interest. The conflict is unresolved as the protagonist leaves without resolving the situation or providing financial support.

Two swindlers get to prison for alcohol.coa:
Two swindlers get to prison for alcohol theft.
After serving their sentence, the two swindlers are released from prison.
They find employment in a lab where they work on inventing a device for regeneration of old cows.
The device is completed and tested successfully, with rejuvenated results on an old cow.

The owner of a salon becomes interested in using the device for human rejuvenation, not just animal.
The swindlers are approached by the salon owner to adapt the device for human use.
They agree to make the necessary modifications to the device.
However, something goes wrong during the modification process.
Two swindlers get to prison for alcohol.outcomes: Protagonists undergo physical transformation from adults back to children. The conflict is unresolved with unintended consequences and new challenges.
Two swindlers get to prison for alcohol.theme: unintended consequences; playing god; identity crisis

A Russian outpost in Eastern Siberia com.coa:
Work begins on a new airfield in a strategically located town in Eastern Siberia.
Tensions develop between workers and a local religious sect due to disagreements over the construction project.
The sect threatens to give their support to a band of marauding samurai warriors who are battling for control of the region.
The Japanese threaten to attack the outpost, further straining relations between Russia and Japan.
A Russian outpost in Eastern Siberia com.outcomes: Protagonist survives the attack and maintains control of the airfield. The conflict is resolved with a victory for the Russians, but broader tensions between nations remain.
A Russian outpost in Eastern Siberia com.theme: patriotism; loyalty vs duty; national defense

Theo and Gustav Benthin are two brothers.coa:
Theo illegally transfers goods to Gustav across the border.
Gustav sells the goods in West Germany.
The People's Police catch Theo, disrupting the smuggling operation.
Gustav's business collapses due to lack of cheap merchandise from the East.
Theo and Gustav Benthin are two brothers.outcomes: Protagonist's smuggling operation is dismantled, but protagonist survives with a new job as a steel worker. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation for some characters, but broader economic and social issues persist.
Theo and Gustav Benthin are two brothers.theme: capitalism vs socialism; loyalty and family ties; adaptation to changing circumstances

A peasant found a devil in his fields, s.coa: A peasant finds a devil sitting on a fire in his fields, which he believes is actually treasure. The devil offers to share the crop with the peasant for two years, but only if half of it belongs to him. The peasant agrees to this proposal, and they come up with a plan to divide the crop so that the half above ground belongs to the devil and the half below ground belongs to the peasant.
A peasant found a devil in his fields, s.outcomes: Protagonist successfully acquires treasure through cunning. The conflict is resolved with the devil's defeat and the protagonist's gain.
A peasant found a devil in his fields, s.theme: cunning vs brute force; cleverness over power; outwitting evil

When Karyn Evans discovers her arrogantl.coa: Karyn discovers her husband is having an affair with a student. She retaliates by starting a relationship with a campus carpenter. Adam learns about Karyn's new relationship and becomes infuriated. The four decide to share a ski house in Vermont, despite their complicated situation.
When Karyn Evans discovers her arrogantl.outcomes: Protagonist achieves temporary satisfaction through revenge but ultimately resolves conflict through family reconciliation. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation as the family comes together to address their issues.

Andrei Dubrovsky is an old poor nobleman.coa: A landowner's property is confiscated by a wealthy aristocrat. The son of the landowner seeks revenge and justice by gathering a group of serfs to steal from the rich and give to the poor. This banditry attracts attention and causes concern among the local population, particularly those who are targeted by the thefts. As a result, the son becomes notorious in the area.
Andrei Dubrovsky is an old poor nobleman.outcomes: Protagonist fails to achieve revenge or justice for his father. The conflict is unresolved with tragic consequences.
Andrei Dubrovsky is an old poor nobleman.theme: revenge vs morality; class struggle; love as weakness

When the Daltons are killed at Coffeyvil.coa: The Dalton gang is killed at Coffeyville, KS. A member of the gang escapes but kills a man in the process. The escaped gang member becomes wanted for murder and takes over as leader of the remaining gang members. The gang member eventually leaves the gang to start a new life under an assumed name.
When the Daltons are killed at Coffeyvil.outcomes: Protagonist's attempt at redemption fails, and he returns to a life of crime. The conflict remains unresolved with ongoing evasion from the law.
When the Daltons are killed at Coffeyvil.theme: reform vs recidivism; identity concealment; fugitive lifestyle

The film depicts a group of peasants who.coa:
A group of peasants arrive at Tolstoy's home seeking land.
Tolstoy attempts to help the peasants while his wife is preoccupied with counting money and arguing.
The stress of the situation overwhelms Tolstoy, driving him to contemplate suicide.
Tolstoy becomes ill and eventually dies in bed.
The film depicts a group of peasants who.outcomes: Tolstoy is shown to be struggling with personal demons, ultimately leading to a symbolic resolution of his inner conflict. The conflict is unresolved as the focus shifts from Tolstoy's struggles to his eventual death.
The film depicts a group of peasants who.theme: struggle vs acceptance; morality in action; human mortality

Based in the Briar Patch (Tikrit Expanse.coa: The story begins on Deep Space 12, a space station in orbit of the planet Ba'ku. The daily life of several officers serving on board the station is introduced. An advanced species, the Grey, are introduced as the primary villain. Their presence sets off a chain of events that spans the entire series.
Based in the Briar Patch (Tikrit Expanse.outcomes: The conflict with the Grey is partially resolved through cooperation with other species, but broader threats remain. The protagonist's understanding of the universe and their place in it undergoes personal transformation.
Based in the Briar Patch (Tikrit Expanse.theme: cooperation vs isolation; ancient knowledge vs modern power; diversity in a complex universe

Twenty-five-year-old Lucile is the beaut.coa:
Lucile meets a charming young man her own age, Antoine.
She falls in love with him and he finds her a menial job in a publishing firm.
She becomes pregnant with his child despite being unable or unwilling to hold down the job.
Antoine accepts Charles' funding of an abortion against his initial wishes.
Twenty-five-year-old Lucile is the beaut.outcomes: Protagonist Lucile transitions back into relationship with Charles and achieves stability. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation, as she prioritizes material security over emotional connection.
Twenty-five-year-old Lucile is the beaut.theme: materialism vs emotional fulfillment; love vs convenience; self-discovery

Steve Randall (Brodie) is an independent.coa:
An independent trucker is hired to haul freight by an old friend.
The trucker arrives at the warehouse and discovers the goods are stolen.
A cop is killed during a burglary, and most of the thieves escape except one.
The remaining thief is captured and sentenced to death for the murder. 
The trucker's wife is kidnapped and assaulted by the criminals.
The criminals threaten to mutilate the trucker's wife unless he confesses to the murder committed by Radak's brother.
The trucker plays along with the criminals' demands in order to escape.
The trucker takes his wife and leaves town, heading cross country.
The couple is pursued by both the cops and the crooks.
The trucker discovers that his wife is pregnant.
Steve Randall (Brodie) is an independent.outcomes: Protagonist escapes danger and achieves safety for himself and his pregnant wife. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing pursuit by both authorities and criminals.
Steve Randall (Brodie) is an independent.theme: survival under pressure; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Six friends come together to try to find.coa:
Six friends come together to make an adult movie with no experience or knowledge of filmmaking.
They complete the film despite having no idea what they're doing.
The crew screens the film for the first time at a local bar.
A brother of a girl who was not used in the final cut interrupts the screening, takes the reel off the projector, and sets it on fire after covering it with whiskey.

This causes all copies of the original film to be destroyed, including the only print they had made.
The cinematographer reveals that he had been videotaping the production process on mini DV tapes throughout.
He edits these tapes into a documentary about making and losing the original film.
The new documentary is released as The Amateurs.
Six friends come together to try to find.outcomes: Protagonists achieve success with their new documentary, The Amateurs. The conflict is resolved with personal and relational transformation through the creation and release of the documentary.

Mecha, a woman in her 50s with several t.coa:
The family spends the summers at their decaying country estate, La Mandrágora, to avoid the hot and humid city weather.
Mecha falls and injures herself, forcing her to stay in bed.
She begins drinking as a result of her confinement.
Her behavior causes tension with her gloomy Amerindian servants, whom she accuses of theft and laziness.
Mecha, a woman in her 50s with several t.outcomes: Protagonist Mecha is unable to escape her physical limitations and emotional turmoil. The conflict is unresolved with potential for further escalation of violence and tension.
Mecha, a woman in her 50s with several t.theme: aging vs youth; class divisions; family secrets

Ha Sang-hyeon is the owner of a hand lau.coa: A baby is left in the church's baby box. The church's surveillance footage shows this event. Dong-soo deletes the footage. Sang-hyeon steals another baby from the baby box.
Ha Sang-hyeon is the owner of a hand lau.outcomes: Protagonist is caught and brought to justice, but not before exposing the larger adoption black market. The conflict is resolved with personal accountability and social reintegration.
Ha Sang-hyeon is the owner of a hand lau.theme: exploitation; moral compromise; consequences of crime

Huey Walker (Dennis Hopper) is a hippie.coa:
A former New Left radical is accused of a crime he did not commit and has been on the run for 20 years.
The FBI sets out to transport him back to Spokane for trial.
An agent is assigned to this task and begins preparing for the journey.
The two cross paths with a corrupt Sheriff who becomes a threat.
Huey Walker (Dennis Hopper) is a hippie.outcomes: Protagonist (Huey Walker) achieves escape from immediate danger with the help of FBI agent John Buckner. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation for both characters, but underlying issues remain.
Huey Walker (Dennis Hopper) is a hippie.theme: redemption; identity vs expectation; human connection

A Chinese immigrant skilled in martial a.coa: A Chinese immigrant skilled in martial arts arrives in America and travels to Texas looking for honest work. He encounters racism wherever he goes. The slave trader Spencer becomes aware of the immigrant's presence and puts a price on his head. This results in the immigrant being targeted by Spencer and his associates.
A Chinese immigrant skilled in martial a.outcomes: Protagonist survives and achieves escape from immediate danger. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader threats remain.
A Chinese immigrant skilled in martial a.theme: survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

A young woman from the country comes to.coa:
The young woman moves to the city to work as a maid in a wealthy household.
She begins working for the family, interacting with its members daily.
She starts an affair with one of the family's children, who is known to be unreliable.
He gets her pregnant, causing tension and conflict within the household.

The child's behavior worsens, but he eventually reforms after realizing the impact of his actions on others.
The young woman gives birth to a child, solidifying their relationship.
They decide to get married in a ceremony attended by family members.
A young woman from the country comes to.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a stable relationship and starts a new life through marriage. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation and personal growth.

After graduating from the École national.coa: Graduates from the École nationale d'administration. Lands a job as speechwriter in the Foreign Ministry. Existing senior advisers do not welcome the newcomer due to potential competition. The Minister recognizes the newcomer's abilities, as does Maupas, the career official heading the department.
After graduating from the École national.outcomes: Protagonist successfully delivers speech on behalf of Minister, contradicting US claims and arguing for disarmament of Iraq. The conflict is resolved with a clear expression of France's position in the international community.
After graduating from the École national.theme: talent vs bureaucracy; loyalty vs ambition; effective communication under pressure

In the lower echelons of Parisian societ.coa: A young woman is forced into a loveless marriage by her aunt. She spends her days confined behind a shop counter and her evenings watching her aunt play dominoes with acquaintances. The woman meets an alluring friend of her husband, and they begin an illicit affair. The affair leads to the death of the woman's husband during an outing on a lake.
In the lower echelons of Parisian societ.outcomes: Protagonist dies by suicide after being caught for murder. The conflict is unresolved with no justice served due to the protagonists' actions.
In the lower echelons of Parisian societ.theme: repressed desire; morality vs passion; fatal consequences

The story follows the decadent heir Henr.coa:
The decadent heir becomes enamored of Paquita Valdes.
He plans to seduce her and succeeds in doing so.
However, his infatuation turns to disillusionment when he discovers she is involved with another lover.
This discovery leads him to plot her murder.
The story follows the decadent heir Henr.outcomes: Paquita's death is confirmed and de Marsay is left with a sense of disillusionment. The conflict is unresolved as de Marsay's actions are foiled by circumstance.
The story follows the decadent heir Henr.theme: objectification; power dynamics; morality vs desire

Lambert a reclusive man lives in isolate.coa: A reclusive man lives in an isolated house on an island in a lake. He becomes obsessed with the idea that someone wants to kill him and appeals for police protection. Inspector Roland is assigned to investigate and arrives on the fog-bound island. The inspector encounters strange happenings, including the death of the local doctor.
Lambert a reclusive man lives in isolate.outcomes: Protagonist's fears are partially validated, but the true nature of the threat remains unclear. The conflict is unresolved with partial personal transformation for the protagonist.
Lambert a reclusive man lives in isolate.theme: paranoia; isolation vs connection; truth vs deception

FBI agent Diane Norwood (Carrere) and DE.coa:
FBI agent Diane Norwood and DEA agent Max Parrish are both in pursuit of a trio of allied crime bosses and a man named Livingston, who handles their finances.
The two agents race to be the first to arrest the criminals and seize their millions in cash in order to claim the money for their agency.
Neither agent can be beaten or persuaded off the case, leading them to reluctantly decide to work together.
They discover an eccentric assassin who works with Livingston, becoming the agents' only link to resolving the case.

She later discovers that Benny was killed in action and has been awarded the Medal of Honor posthumously.

The employer hopes that the waiter will use his newfound wealth to buy out of the contract with a large payment. The waiter refuses to pay and continues working at the cafe.

He meets up with his old friend who owns a venue on the Reeperbahn.

He sets out on the sea voyage, carrying the necklace.

A white-collar suburban father Kyle (Fra.coa: A long-lost college buddy appears unexpectedly at the office. A woman gives him shelter, treats his wounds, and feeds him before sending him back out into the wilderness.

The donkey's years are lessened because it bears heavy burdens.

He was impressed by a stately home and a large ship laden with precious commodities.
He innocently asked people about the owners of the house and the boat, and both times received the answer "Kannitverstan".
He believed that it was the name of a man called "Kannitverstan" and was impressed by his supposed wealth.
A young workman from Tuttlingen (then pa.outcomes: Protagonist gains new perspective on wealth and poverty, and feels better able to cope with his own circumstances. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and a shift in outlook.
A young workman from Tuttlingen (then pa.theme: social class; impermanence of wealth; acceptance

The story revolves around seven-year-old.coa:
Barbro discovers she has a secret twin sister named Ylva-li, who calls her Most Beloved Sister.
Ylva-li tells Barbro that she will die when the roses on Salikon wither.
Barbro refuses to believe Ylva-li and returns to her parents.
The next day, the roses on Salikon are dead.
The story revolves around seven-year-old.outcomes: Protagonist (Barbro) experiences loss and separation from twin sister Ylva-li. The conflict is unresolved as the magical world is lost, but Barbro's relationship with her parents is superficially restored.
The story revolves around seven-year-old.theme: loss of innocence; sibling bond; impermanence

In a Santiago motel room, two young midd.coa: Two young middle-class individuals engage in lovemaking in a motel room. They had met while leaving a party and don't know each other's names. As the night progresses, they continue to have sex and eventually reveal their names to each other; he is Bruno and she is Daniela. During intimate moments, they share more details about their lives, sorrows, and fears.
In a Santiago motel room, two young midd.outcomes: Protagonist survives the encounter without emotional harm or physical consequences. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.
In a Santiago motel room, two young midd.theme: transience of relationships; vulnerability in intimacy; authenticity vs deception

Eva Norová goes to visit her aunt Pa for.coa: Eva visits her aunt for her 60th birthday. Aunt Pa expresses a desire to learn how to grow a specific type of roses that Záhorský has cultivated. Eva applies for a job as a secretary at Záhorský's residence in order to steal the instructions for growing the roses. Meanwhile, Michal arrives at the Záhorský residence with his own intentions.
Eva Norová goes to visit her aunt Pa for.outcomes: Protagonist achieves reconciliation between aunt and neighbours, and finds romantic partner. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation and social reintegration.
Eva Norová goes to visit her aunt Pa for.theme: reconciliation; deception vs honesty; love in unexpected places

Mandy, a 16-year old girl, receives text.coa:
A 16-year-old girl receives text messages from friends about a viral video featuring her unconscious in a compromising situation with a group of laughing boys.
The video has gone viral, causing widespread attention and ridicule among the girl's peers.
As the girl tries to figure out what happened, she is ostracized by her friends who are now embarrassed to be associated with her.
Her attempts to investigate further lead to more people discovering the video, increasing her isolation.
Mandy, a 16-year old girl, receives text.outcomes: Protagonist uncovers truth about past incident and begins healing process. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing social isolation.

In a quest to find a source of radioacti.coa:
A four-man mission to the Moon on the rocketship Stardust is launched.
The mission finds a stranded Arkonide spaceship on the Moon, where Commander Thora is trying to save a scientist and robots.
Crest's condition is discovered to be leukemia, for which there is a cure available on Earth.
Perry and others take an Arkonide shuttlecraft to Earth to bring back a doctor with the cure.
In a quest to find a source of radioacti.outcomes: Protagonist succeeds in rescuing Thora and curing Crest. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation for Crest and the protagonist's team, as well as social reintegration through the exchange of technology.
In a quest to find a source of radioacti.theme: cooperation vs exploitation; technological advancement; interstellar cooperation

Karla Zachanassian, a fabulously wealthy.coa: A wealthy woman returns to a decaying village she was forced to desert years earlier in disgrace. She had borne a child by a man who denied paternity and now seeks revenge against him. The townspeople are initially reluctant, but eventually agree to arrange for the man's death in exchange for a vast sum of money. They condemn him to death, preparing for his execution.
Karla Zachanassian, a fabulously wealthy.outcomes: The conflict is unresolved with partial resolution through guilt and knowledge of villagers' actions. Protagonist achieves escape from immediate situation but leaves behind potential long-term consequences for Anya's life.
Karla Zachanassian, a fabulously wealthy.theme: guilt; loyalty vs material gain; the weight of collective responsibility

Frustrated by the repetitious grind of o.coa: Rick experiences frustration with one-night stands and aimless hustling, leading him to seek meaning in his life. He sells small amounts of marijuana to cover expenses, insisting he's not doing it for a living. Rick meets Heather, the girl next door, and perceives a new opportunity for a committed relationship. This sparks tension between Rick and his friends, who are threatened by his desire to settle down.
Frustrated by the repetitious grind of o.outcomes: Protagonist fails to establish a committed relationship due to unresolved personal issues. The conflict is unresolved with potential for future growth and change.
Frustrated by the repetitious grind of o.theme: searching for identity; struggle between old and new values; tension between desire for stability and need for self-expression

Julian (Kim Joohyuk) has lived off with.coa:
A hasty expansion of his business leaves him with usurious debts from creditors.
He faces death unless he clears the debt in one month.
The only way to save himself is to pretend to be the long-lost brother of an heiress and kill her to get her huge fortune.
He meets the blind heiress, who slowly opens herself to him.
Julian (Kim Joohyuk) has lived off with.outcomes: Protagonist is left emotionally scarred by his actions and the consequences that follow. The conflict is unresolved with a focus on personal transformation and emotional turmoil.
Julian (Kim Joohyuk) has lived off with.theme: guilt; deception vs morality; human cost of ambition

In the early 1970s, while the majority o.coa:
The United States Army secretly develops a way to resurrect and control dead bodies in the early 1970s.
The experiments are shut down when the reanimated corpses are unable to control their hunger for human flesh.
Thirty years later, the army decides to reopen the project.
They choose Grover City as the location for the main testing facilities due to its remote location.
In the early 1970s, while the majority o.outcomes: Protagonist group survives and finds temporary solution to zombie outbreak. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threat of zombie attacks.
In the early 1970s, while the majority o.theme: survival under adversity; control vs nature; human resilience

Vincent Malivert is the head of a presti.coa: The head of a prestigious jewel broker's firm commits suicide due to debt and implication in trafficking stolen jewels. His wife discovers a set of perfect cut diamonds in his safe after his death. She decides to use this opportunity to rebuild her life despite knowing the diamonds are probably stolen. She sets about trying to find a buyer for the hidden jewels, which leads her into contact with a shady dealer.
Vincent Malivert is the head of a presti.outcomes: Marianne survives financial crisis and begins rebuilding life. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal struggles and potential consequences from dealing with a shady character.

The original manuscript opens with the m.coa: The man laments that his ba is disobeying him by restraining him from death. He expresses a desire to reach the West (the afterlife) and rebukes his ba for preventing this. The man promises a bountiful burial and prosperity in death to persuade his ba towards death. His ba responds by reminding him of the sadness of death and its equalizing effect on all men, regardless of wealth or legacy.
The original manuscript opens with the m.outcomes: Protagonist is left unchanged in his perspective but continues with his current path. The conflict remains unresolved as the protagonist persists in seeking death and the afterlife.
The original manuscript opens with the m.theme: acceptance of mortality; fleeting nature of human legacy; balance between life and death

As the end of the Second World War appro.coa: The Soviet Red Army advances as World War II nears its end. A group of concentration camp inmates is helped to escape by a Polish doctor. They hide in a wood where they meet other fugitives who have been there for months, constantly in fear of being discovered. The food supplies run low due to their inability to leave the forest out of fear of German army patrols.
As the end of the Second World War appro.outcomes: Protagonist group survives and escapes danger. The conflict is resolved with external intervention.
As the end of the Second World War appro.theme: survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

The story of the forty-seven rōnin has b.coa: Lord Asano assaults Lord Kira, leading to his immediate sentence of seppuku by the shogun. The shogun's decision is based on the severity of the assault. Twenty-one months pass after Lord Asano's sentence, during which time the forty-seven loyal retainers prepare for revenge against Lord Kira. The retainers launch a surprise attack on Kira's castle.
The story of the forty-seven rōnin has b.outcomes: Protagonist is deceased, but his honor is upheld through the actions of his loyal retainers. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation for the retainers, who demonstrate bushido principles.

Blayde \"Reb\" Hollister is a former Con.coa: A group of carpetbaggers murders a family and destroys their home in Georgia. The family's son fakes his own death with the help of Wild Bill Hickok. He swaps identities with an inexperienced Federal Marshal from the Eastern United States, who is using the position to impress his fiancée.
Blayde \"Reb\" Hollister is a former Con.outcomes: Protagonist (Reb) achieves revenge against the carpetbaggers, resolving a personal conflict. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as Reb finds closure for his past trauma.

Landowner Morton wants to expand his pro.coa:
A peace treaty is signed between the settlers and the Comanches, providing security for the area.
Settlers begin to move into the Indian territory, unaware of the oil deposits under it.
The landowner discovers the presence of oil deposits and decides to expand his property at any cost.
He disguises himself as a soldier and orders his men to attack the Indians.
Landowner Morton wants to expand his pro.outcomes: The conflict is resolved with the exposure of Morton's deceit and the defeat of his men. Protagonist (the settlers) survives and achieves safety through the assistance of the Indians and Captain Jackson.
Landowner Morton wants to expand his pro.theme: deception vs truth; colonialism; loyalty vs duty

Jane Froman (Susan Hayward) is a humble.coa:
A staff singer at a Cincinnati radio station rises to network radio fame due to unknown factors.
She marries her agent, who facilitated her rise to fame.
Both parties realize they're not truly in love with each other.
Her popularity soars further, leading to an invitation for a European tour.
Jane Froman (Susan Hayward) is a humble.outcomes: Protagonist survives and maintains career despite physical limitations. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and continued success.
Jane Froman (Susan Hayward) is a humble.theme: resilience; love vs convenience; overcoming adversity

Music by Prudence tells a self-empowerin.coa:
A severely disabled young woman is born into a society where disabilities are associated with witchcraft.
Many disabled children in this society are abandoned due to these superstitions.
The young woman forms a band called Liyana with seven other young people who are also disabled.
Together, they face and overcome the stereotypes and prejudices of their community.
Music by Prudence tells a self-empowerin.outcomes: Protagonist achieves self-empowerment and conveys her message of ability despite disability to the world. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social change.
Music by Prudence tells a self-empowerin.theme: disability vs stigma; empowerment through art; overcoming societal expectations

A barber shop owner travels to Vienna to.coa:
A barber shop owner travels to Vienna to receive his inheritance from his late aunt.
He finds that all she has left him are thirteen old chairs.
Needing to raise enough money to pay for his ticket back home, he sells them to a second-hand dealer.
The dealer breaks down the chairs and resells their parts to various people in the city.
One of the new owners discovers a letter from the barber shop owner's aunt hidden inside one of the chairs.
A barber shop owner travels to Vienna to.outcomes: Protagonist successfully recovers the hidden inheritance and returns home with financial stability. The conflict is resolved with personal gain but broader financial uncertainty remains.

The police prepares to trap the Drug Lor.coa:
The police prepares a trap for the Drug Lord of Naples.
The plan fails, and Rizzo decides to interrogate the Drug Lord for the last time.
Rizzo arrives at the location, but finds that the Drug Lord has been murdered.
Rizzo is accused of the murder.
The police prepares to trap the Drug Lor.outcomes: Rizzo clears his name and identifies the corrupt police senior. The conflict is resolved with personal vindication but broader corruption remains.
The police prepares to trap the Drug Lor.theme: corruption; loyalty vs duty; justice vs power

Vollmöller's play wordlessly tells the s.coa: A wayward nun deserts her convent with a knight. The music of an evil minstrel influences their actions. A statue of the Virgin Mary comes to life and takes the physical place of the nun. The nun, now in disguise as the statue, navigates the world and faces various challenges. She is eventually accused of witchcraft but manages to escape. The nun returns to her convent with a dying infant.
Vollmöller's play wordlessly tells the s.outcomes: Protagonist is forgiven by authority and reconciled with her community. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and redemption.
Vollmöller's play wordlessly tells the s.theme: redemption; identity crisis; faith vs temptation

The film tells the story of two Shinseng.coa:
The Tokugawa shogunate falls.
A series of flashbacks are told as two men reminisce about their past.
During this time, one samurai is portrayed as a heartless killer and the other as an emotional swordsman from Nambu Morioka.
These portrayals contrast with each other, highlighting conflicting loyalties.
The film tells the story of two Shinseng.outcomes: Protagonist's actions are justified as a means to protect his family. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and acceptance of difficult choices.
The film tells the story of two Shinseng.theme: loyalty vs duty; family obligations; moral complexity

Timothy Benson, a young intellectual and.coa:
A young intellectual inherits a gold mine.
The bandido leader, Rodrigo, learns of the inheritance and decides to take control of the mine for himself.
Rodrigo sends his men to intimidate the new owner into deeding him the mine.
The foreman and his friends witness the intimidation attempt and decide to intervene on behalf of the young intellectual.
Timothy Benson, a young intellectual and.outcomes: Protagonist successfully defends mine against antagonist, achieving personal growth through mentorship. The conflict is resolved with conflict_resolved and personal transformation.
Timothy Benson, a young intellectual and.theme: survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Jerome Littlefield is an orderly at a ho.coa: The orderly works at a hospital where patients are being treated for various ailments. He develops a condition where he psychosomatically experiences the same symptoms as his patients when he learns of their problems. An old high school friend is brought to the hospital after attempting to take her own life, and the orderly recognizes her as someone he has had feelings for since high school. The orderly begins to develop romantic feelings for the patient again.
Jerome Littlefield is an orderly at a ho.outcomes: Protagonist becomes a successful doctor. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and emotional healing.
Jerome Littlefield is an orderly at a ho.theme: overcoming unrequited love; the power of relationships to heal; self-discovery through love

The film follows five simultaneous, loos.coa:
A man kills his wife and buries her in the woods, but she is found alive when he returns home.
The suburban family's life is disrupted when the father is hypnotized into believing he is a bird.
The thuggish hitman and his translator intervene in one of the plot lines, causing chaos and confusion.
The aimless youth are hired by the hitman to burglarize houses.
The film follows five simultaneous, loos.outcomes: Protagonist's marriage is irreparably damaged; father remains hypnotized with no clear resolution; aimless youth are unrepentant; advertising executive's creativity is stifled; hitman's intervention escalates conflict.
The film follows five simultaneous, loos.theme: chaos; identity crisis; the absurdity of modern life

Marius and Jeannette live in the same wo.coa:
The company that owns the abandoned cement works goes out of business and announces plans to demolish the plant, forcing Marius to squat on the property to save money.
Marius catches Jeannette trying to steal paint from the cement lot and tries to chase her away.
Jeannette is caught by Marius and attempts to flee with the stolen paint.
The next day, Marius visits Jeannette's apartment to apologize for his behavior the previous day and brings her the two cans of paint he had confiscated.
Marius and Jeannette live in the same wo.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a new romantic connection and begins to heal from past emotional trauma. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relational growth.
Marius and Jeannette live in the same wo.theme: healing through relationships; love as redemption; overcoming past wounds

Frank Fogle, a widower, reluctantly emba.coa: A widower embarks on a journey to spread his wife's ashes in a remote lake. His estranged son is released from prison and plans to travel to the West Coast, but his travel arrangements collapse. The son reluctantly agrees to accompany his father on the trip in exchange for a ticket to the West Coast. They set off together, with the son intending to part ways as soon as they reach their destination.
Frank Fogle, a widower, reluctantly emba.outcomes: Protagonist achieves closure on his wife's last wish, and he and his son begin to reconnect. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation and relational improvement.
Frank Fogle, a widower, reluctantly emba.theme: healing through connection; redemption of family relationships; letting go of the past

Susan (who initially calls herself Rita).coa:
A 26-year-old working-class hairdresser signs up for an Open University course in Literature.
She is assigned a tutor who has given up on teaching and turned to drinking.
The tutor's passion for literature is rekindled by the student's enthusiasm, despite her limited technical ability.
The tutor initially doubts the student's ability to adapt to university culture but is impressed by her verve and earnestness.
Susan (who initially calls herself Rita).outcomes: Protagonist achieves self-improvement through education, but also experiences disillusionment with the superficiality of her new social environment. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation, but broader issues remain.
Susan (who initially calls herself Rita).theme: self-discovery; superficiality vs authenticity; intellectual vs emotional growth

Windstorm is the story of the teenage gi.coa:
A teenage girl accidentally sets fire to her teacher's car.
As a result, she gets sent to her grandmother's stable over the holidays.
While at the stable, she learns that she has a natural talent for riding and can understand the language of the horses.
She discovers that she is the only one who can ride and tame Windstorm, a dangerous horse.
Windstorm is the story of the teenage gi.outcomes: Protagonist finds success and self-improvement through her newfound abilities. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and a new sense of purpose.

The film examines the eternal problem of.coa: A sound engineer and musician encounters many problems and setbacks in his work and personal life. A Canadian company invites him to record sounds of animals in Transcarpathia. He fulfills the assignment with enthusiasm, but his mother comes along and disrupts his work. The engineer is tasked with recording the voice of a rare Rakhiv mallard.
The film examines the eternal problem of.outcomes: Protagonist successfully completes assignment and gains opportunity to travel to Canada. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal problems.
The film examines the eternal problem of.theme: parent-child relationships; creative fulfillment vs personal struggles; leaving the past behind

A decade ago, agent Martin Baxter quit M.coa: A journalist asks for help solving an old case, prompting a former agent to re-enter the world of espionage. The journalist's request puts the former agent in the crosshairs of UK and Russian intelligence agencies. The former agent learns that their daughter is being held captive by the KGB as leverage. This information creates a time-sensitive situation, with only 24 hours to complete a task.
A decade ago, agent Martin Baxter quit M.outcomes: Protagonist delivers case files, but broader implications for his family and allies remain uncertain. The conflict is unresolved with potential for further danger.
A decade ago, agent Martin Baxter quit M.theme: trauma; loyalty vs duty; collateral damage

Stefano (Lino Capolicchio) arrives in a.coa: Stefano arrives in a village where he has been employed to restore a fresco. He temporarily takes up residence in a house previously owned by two sisters of a deceased painter named Legnani. Stefano begins a romance with a new schoolteacher, Francesca, while learning from townspeople that the painter was a madman who derived his art from real life. The townspeople reveal that the artist and his sisters were killers who tortured people to death for inspiration, including for the fresco Stefano is restoring.
Stefano (Lino Capolicchio) arrives in a.outcomes: Protagonist survives and uncovers the truth behind the artist's legacy. The conflict is unresolved with broader threats remaining.
Stefano (Lino Capolicchio) arrives in a.theme: artistic inspiration vs morality; darkness in human nature; the power of art

Both of her parents now dead, the young.coa:
Vera returns to her estate after both her parents have died.
Her aunt now runs the estate, which Vera finds quiet but also unbearably dull.
As time passes, Vera becomes increasingly disillusioned with life at the estate due to her aunt's insincerity and provincial behavior, as well as the grandfather's rudeness and gluttony.
The local doctor Neshchapov is introduced, described as vacuous and bland, and it is suggested that he has a romantic interest in Vera.
Both of her parents now dead, the young.outcomes: Protagonist is trapped in an unhappy marriage, sacrificing personal desires for stability. The conflict is unresolved with a sense of resignation and compromise.
Both of her parents now dead, the young.theme: unfulfilled dreams; suffocating social expectations; compromised happiness

As Miguel de Cervantes and his manservan.coa:
Cervantes and his manservant wait to be called before the Spanish Inquisition for putting on a play critical of the tribunal.
The fellow inmates subject Cervantes to a sham trial, taking all of his possessions as their own concern is only the manuscript he has with him.
Cervantes mounts his defense in the form of a play, where he takes the role of Alonso Quijana, an elderly gentleman who believes he should go forth as a knight-errant.
Quijana renames himself Don Quixote de La Mancha and sets out with his "squire", Sancho Panza.
As Miguel de Cervantes and his manservan.outcomes: Protagonist (Cervantes) survives and achieves creative expression through storytelling. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation for Quijana, but broader societal issues remain.
As Miguel de Cervantes and his manservan.theme: reality vs fantasy; the power of imagination; human resilience

The old grandmother Tina arrives in town.coa: The old grandmother arrives in town for a wedding. She discovers that her medallion has been stolen upon arrival. She goes to the police station to file a complaint about the theft. The investigation is too slow, so she decides to search for the thief herself.
The old grandmother Tina arrives in town.outcomes: Protagonist successfully retrieves stolen item and sees family event proceed as planned. The conflict is resolved with personal satisfaction and community recognition.

Set in the 1890s, the story centers arou.coa: A powerful railroad and land baron plans to build a new railroad by acquiring control of local properties. He targets several families in the area, including the Collogeros, for their lands. The conflict escalates when he murders the patriarch of one of these families. In response, his son claims justice and starts an open guerrilla war against the land baron with the help of his family and others.
Set in the 1890s, the story centers arou.outcomes: Protagonist achieves partial resolution of immediate threat through confrontation but broader systemic issues remain. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation as protagonist takes action to protect family.
Set in the 1890s, the story centers arou.theme: land ownership vs corporate power; family loyalty vs external threats; resistance against oppression

Poor soldier, Ludvig Kahlen, arrives in.coa: A poor soldier arrives in 1755 on the barren Jutland heath to cultivate the land. The soldier makes an enemy when a serf runs away with his wife and seeks refuge with the soldier, which is seen as a betrayal by the landowner who believes the heath belongs to him. The landowner tries to drive the soldier away and exact revenge on the family that has taken in the fugitive couple.
Poor soldier, Ludvig Kahlen, arrives in.outcomes: Protagonist survives and maintains bond with the serf family despite life-threatening risks. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing tension between protagonist and antagonist.
Poor soldier, Ludvig Kahlen, arrives in.theme: land ownership vs authority; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Antonio, a brazen, individualistic ETA t.coa: The ETA terrorist travels with two fellow cell members to Madrid to carry out an attack on a police station. They intend to use a car bomb containing 100 kg of explosives. The terrorist moves into the area under the guise of a photographer, and meets his neighbor, a naive prostitute with an impending drug problem. He falls for her and uses her desire to have their first tryst in Granada as an excuse to flee Madrid right after he shoots a police officer.
Antonio, a brazen, individualistic ETA t.outcomes: Protagonist dies in the explosion, sacrificing himself for love and possibly redemption. The conflict is unresolved as broader threats remain.
Antonio, a brazen, individualistic ETA t.theme: self-destruction; loyalty vs duty; human cost of terrorism

Sandy McKenzie (Stewart Granger) sets ou.coa:
McKenzie sets out on his last hunt with his new partner.
Gilson derives pleasure from killing entire herds of buffalo at one time and chases down an Indian raiding party.
The Indian woman and her child are taken captive by Gilson.
The presence of the native woman causes tension between McKenzie and Gilson, leading to a decline in Gilson's mental state.
Sandy McKenzie (Stewart Granger) sets ou.outcomes: Protagonist survives and achieves escape from immediate danger. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as McKenzie emerges unscathed, but his partner's demise serves as a catalyst for change.
Sandy McKenzie (Stewart Granger) sets ou.theme: survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

A young man, Juvenal, is apparently able.coa:
A former evangelist is dissatisfied with his current occupation of selling mobile homes.
He convinces a friend to befriend an innocent ex-monk who has a unique ability to cure the sick by laying on hands.
The friend befriends the ex-monk and encourages him to pursue a more prominent role.
The young couple falls in love, causing complications.
A young man, Juvenal, is apparently able.outcomes: Protagonist's relationships are strained but ultimately resolved through confrontation of antagonist. The conflict is partially resolved with relational transformation and social reintegration.
A young man, Juvenal, is apparently able.theme: faith vs doubt; love vs prejudice; authenticity

Kim Yool is a detective who attempts to.coa: A detective attempts to kill mob boss Carlos Kun but gets arrested and transferred to Sura Island, a prison for notorious criminals. On the island, the detective learns that Carlos is also serving his term there. The detective meets a child Jin and his mother Maly, who initially despise him due to past imprisonment.
Kim Yool is a detective who attempts to.outcomes: Protagonist achieves revenge for family's death by killing antagonist. The conflict is resolved with protagonist escaping the island and antagonist defeated.

The extraordinary novel tells the story.coa: A disease wipes out the African supply of silkworms, prompting a merchant to travel to Japan for a new source. The merchant arrives in Japan during the Bakumatsu period, when the country is largely closed to foreigners. The merchant becomes involved in trade and forms a personal relationship with the concubine of a local baron. Internal political turmoil and growing anti-Western sentiment in Japan strain both the merchant's trade and his relationship with the concubine.
The extraordinary novel tells the story.outcomes: Protagonist fails to establish a stable trade relationship and returns home. The conflict is unresolved due to external circumstances beyond his control, with no personal transformation or resolution.
The extraordinary novel tells the story.theme: cultural clash; isolationism vs globalization; obsession

A woman lives alone on the outskirts of.coa: A woman receives a parcel she had sent to her incarcerated husband, marked 'return to sender'. The parcel's contents cause her shock and confusion. She decides to travel to the prison where the parcel was returned from in search of an explanation. Her journey begins as she sets out towards the remote region of the country where the prison is located.
A woman lives alone on the outskirts of.outcomes: Protagonist uncovers truth about prison's corrupt practices and secures justice for her husband. The conflict is resolved with systemic change, exposing corruption within the prison system.
A woman lives alone on the outskirts of.theme: justice vs oppression; resilience in adversity; fighting against institutional evil
